title: Chinese integrators capture 76% of global BESS market as dominance expands
gist: Chinese system integrators captured 76% of the global battery energy storage system market in 2025 with eight of the top ten integrators now headquartered in China; Tesla and Sungrow held the top two spots for a third year, BYD surged, and competition is shifting from scale to policy compliance, grid-forming tech, software and financing amid tightening US (OBBBA/FEOC) and EU import rules.

insights.claim: Chinese system integrators captured 76% of the global battery energy storage system market in 2025, with eight of the top 10 global integrators now headquartered in China.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: China's grip on BESS integration is a supply-chain concentration that Western policy is actively trying to counter; a structural competitive fact.
insights.locator: Chinese system integrators continued to dominate

insights.claim: Tesla and Sungrow retained the top two global integrator positions for the third consecutive year while BYD advanced five places to claim third.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Tesla is the lone Western holdout at the top; BYD's rapid climb signals intensifying Chinese competition just below.
insights.locator: China has expanded its global BESS footprint

insights.claim: The combined share of the top three integrators fell from 36% in 2024 to 30% in 2025, but this reflects rapid overall market growth and the rise of mid-tier vendors rather than any weakening at the top.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Falling concentration is a growth signal, not decline; the market is broadening with more viable mid-tier suppliers.
insights.quote: this reflects rapid overall market growth and the rise of mid-tier vendors rather than any weakening at the top
insights.locator: The combined share of the top three integrators fell

themes.theme: North America: Tesla leads amid tariff/policy walls

insights.claim: Tesla retained North American BESS leadership on its Megapack hardware, Autobidder software and US-based manufacturing, while NextEra Energy entered the top three for the first time as a vertically integrated self-integrator insulated from tariff disruption.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Vertical integration and domestic manufacturing are becoming decisive North American advantages as tariffs bite.
insights.locator: Tesla holds firm at the top of the North American

insights.claim: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act introduces FEOC compliance thresholds for the 48E Investment Tax Credit from 2026, with non-FEOC sourcing requirements starting at 55% of project costs in 2026 and rising to 75% by 2030, significantly constraining Chinese manufacturers' ability to supply the US market.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Escalating domestic-content thresholds progressively lock Chinese suppliers out of US tax-credit projects; a tailwind for non-FEOC players.
insights.locator: Looking ahead, the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA)

themes.theme: Europe: Chinese sweep and policy pushback

insights.claim: In Europe Sungrow retained first place, BYD jumped from fifth to second and Huawei entered the top three, giving Chinese vendors all three leading positions, while growth broadens beyond the UK, Germany and Italy to a second tier of Bulgaria, Romania, Belgium, Spain, the Netherlands and Greece.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Chinese vendors dominate Europe as the addressable market widens into new Eastern/Southern European geographies.
insights.locator: Sungrow retains first place in Europe

insights.claim: European policy pressure is intensifying through the Net Zero Industry Act, the Industrial Accelerator Act and the EU Batteries Regulation, all raising the bar for import-dependent suppliers and rewarding local manufacturing capacity.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: EU regulation is tilting toward local content; import-reliant Chinese suppliers face rising non-tariff barriers over time.

themes.theme: APAC, Middle East and Latin America frontiers

insights.claim: CRRC topped APAC for the third consecutive year followed by HyperStrong and Envision, with China accounting for roughly 85% of the APAC market, and Australia remaining the region's most accessible market for non-Chinese integrators given its stringent technical standards and lender preferences.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Australia's standards create a rare non-Chinese-accessible market; Southeast Asia is the emerging growth frontier to watch.

insights.claim: The Middle East entered mega-scale procurement with the UAE announcing a 5.2 GW solar project paired with 19 GWh of storage and Saudi Arabia's two BESS tenders covering a combined 5 GW/20 GWh, with BYD and Sungrow together capturing 87% of the regional market.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Gulf mega-tenders are a large new demand pool that BYD and Sungrow are already dominating at 87% share.
insights.locator: Chinese integrators extend their reach across the Middle East

insights.claim: In Latin America Chile leads with the most mature regulatory framework, and BYD's total contracted volume with Grenergy reached 6.5 GWh across all phases of the Oasis de Atacama project.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Chile is the LatAm BESS leader; the Oasis de Atacama scale shows Chinese integrators winning flagship regional projects.

insights.claim: Competition in the global BESS integrator market is shifting from scale alone to a multidimensional set of capabilities, with policy compliance, grid-forming technology, software-driven revenue optimisation and financing support becoming decisive competitive variables.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Winners will be integrators that combine compliance, grid-forming tech, software and financing; pure volume no longer wins.
insights.quote: Competition in the global BESS integrator market is shifting from scale alone to a multidimensional set of capabilities.
insights.locator: Competition in the global BESS integrator market is shifting

insights.claim: Integrators that can meet demands across diverse regulatory environments will build the most durable market positions through the remainder of the decade.
insights.speaker: Wood Mackenzie
insights.so_what: Regulatory adaptability is the moat for the rest of the decade; favor integrators with multi-jurisdiction compliance depth.
insights.locator: Integrators that can meet these demands
